<!-- BEGIN:nextjs-agent-rules -->
# This is NOT the Next.js you know## Measure Workflow

## Measure Workflow

All development runs through the **Measure** spec-driven development framework exclusively. At the start of every session:

1. Load the `measure` skill
2. Read `measure/index.md` to understand the project context
3. Follow the workflow defined in `measure/workflow.md`

Key reference files:
- `measure/tracks.md` — Active work registry
- `measure/tracks/<track_id>/plan.md` — Task checklist
- `measure/product.md` — Product vision
- `measure/tech-stack.md` — Technology choices
- `measure/lessons-learned.md` — Project memory
- `measure/tech-debt.md` — Known shortcuts

Never start significant work without an active track. Always update `measure/tracks.md` and the current track's `plan.md` before and after work.

type RunDetail = { id: string; model: string; harness: string; harness_version: string; benchmark_version: string; run_date: string; wall_time_seconds: number; total_score: number; scores: { functional_correctness: number; integration_quality: number; regression_safety: number; minimality: number; process_quality: number; }; test_results: { suite: string; name: string; status: "pass" | "fail" | "skip"; duration_ms?: number | undefined; error_message?: string | undefined; }[]; artifacts: { name: string; type: "log" | "screenshot" | "report" | "other"; url: string; size_bytes?: number | undefined; }[]; provider?: string | undefined; dataset_version?: string | undefined; task_id?: string | undefined; task_domain?: string | undefined; diff?: string | undefined; track?: "fixed" | "native" | undefined; }

</types>

<path>/tmp/ralb-added-opencode-go-qwen37-max-dataset-filter/worktree/src/lib/export.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import type { RunDetail } from './schemas';
2: 
3: export interface QueryFilters {
4:   model?: string;
5:   task?: string;
6:   startDate?: string;
7:   endDate?: string;
8:   minScore?: number;
9:   maxScore?: number;
10: }
11: 
12: export interface SummaryStats {
13:   mean: number;
14:   median: number;
15:   p95: number;
16: }
17: 
18: export function filterRuns(runs: RunDetail[], filters: QueryFilters): RunDetail[] {
19:   return runs.filter(run => {
20:     if (filters.model && run.model !== filters.model) {
21:       return false;
22:     }
23:     if (filters.task && run.task_id !== filters.task) {
24:       return false;
25:     }
26:     if (filters.startDate && run.run_date < filters.startDate) {
27:       return false;
28:     }
29:     if (filters.endDate && run.run_date > filters.endDate) {
30:       return false;
31:     }
32:     if (filters.minScore !== undefined && run.total_score < filters.minScore) {
33:       return false;
34:     }
35:     if (filters.maxScore !== undefined && run.total_score > filters.maxScore) {
36:       return false;
37:     }
38:     return true;
39:   });
40: }
41: 
42: export function computeStats(scores: number[]): SummaryStats {
43:   if (scores.length === 0) {
44:     return { mean: 0, median: 0, p95: 0 };
45:   }
46: 
47:   const sorted = [...scores].sort((a, b) => a - b);
48:   const sum = sorted.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
49:   const mean = sum / sorted.length;
50: 
51:   const mid = Math.floor(sorted.length / 2);
52:   const median = sorted.length % 2 !== 0
53:     ? sorted[mid]
54:     : (sorted[mid - 1] + sorted[mid]) / 2;
55: 
56:   const p95Index = Math.ceil(sorted.length * 0.95) - 1;
57:   const p95 = sorted[p95Index];
58: 
59:   return { mean, median, p95 };
60: }
61: 
62: export function exportToCSV(runs: RunDetail[]): string {
63:   const headers = ['id', 'model', 'run_date', 'total_score', 'task_id', 'harness', 'wall_time_seconds'];
64:   const lines: string[] = [headers.join(',')];
65: 
66:   for (const run of runs) {
67:     const row = [
68:       escapeCSV(run.id),
69:       escapeCSV(run.model),
70:       escapeCSV(run.run_date),
71:       run.total_score.toString(),
72:       escapeCSV(run.task_id || ''),
73:       escapeCSV(run.harness),
74:       run.wall_time_seconds.toString(),
75:     ];
76:     lines.push(row.join(','));
77:   }
78: 
79:   return lines.join('\n');
80: }
81: 
82: function escapeCSV(value: string): string {
83:   if (value.includes(',') || value.includes('"') || value.includes('\n')) {
84:     return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
85:   }
86:   return value;
87: }
88: 
89: export function runsToLeaderboardFormat(runs: RunDetail[]) {
90:   const byModel = new Map<string, RunDetail[]>();
91:   for (const run of runs) {
92:     const existing = byModel.get(run.model) || [];
93:     existing.push(run);
94:     byModel.set(run.model, existing);
95:   }
96: 
97:   return Array.from(byModel.entries()).map(([model, modelRuns]) => {
98:     const scores = modelRuns.map(r => r.total_score);
99:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
100:     return {
101:       model,
102:       runs: modelRuns.length,
103:       latestRunDate: modelRuns[0].run_date,
104:       meanScore: stats.mean,
105:       medianScore: stats.median,
106:       p95Score: stats.p95,
107:     };
108:   });
109: }

(End of file - total 109 lines)
</content>

<path>/tmp/ralb-added-opencode-go-qwen37-max-dataset-filter/worktree/app/api/runs/route.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server';
2: import { getAllRuns } from '@/lib/runs';
3: import { filterRuns, type QueryFilters } from '@/lib/export';
4: import { validateAuth, authResponse } from '@/lib/api-auth';
5: 
6: export async function GET(request: NextRequest) {
7:   const authResult = await validateAuth(request);
8:   if (!authResult.allowed && authResult.status) {
9:     return authResponse(authResult.error!, authResult.status);
10:   }
11: 
12:   try {
13:     const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url);
14: 
15:     const filters: QueryFilters = {};
16:     if (searchParams.has('model')) filters.model = searchParams.get('model')!;
17:     if (searchParams.has('task')) filters.task = searchParams.get('task')!;
18:     if (searchParams.has('startDate')) filters.startDate = searchParams.get('startDate')!;
19:     if (searchParams.has('endDate')) filters.endDate = searchParams.get('endDate')!;
20:     if (searchParams.has('minScore')) filters.minScore = parseFloat(searchParams.get('minScore')!);
21:     if (searchParams.has('maxScore')) filters.maxScore = parseFloat(searchParams.get('maxScore')!);
22: 
23:     const limit = searchParams.has('limit') ? parseInt(searchParams.get('limit')!, 10) : 100;
24:     const offset = searchParams.has('offset') ? parseInt(searchParams.get('offset')!, 10) : 0;
25: 
26:     const runs = await getAllRuns();
27:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
28:     const total = filtered.length;
29:     const paginated = filtered.slice(offset, offset + limit);
30: 
31:     return NextResponse.json({
32:       count: paginated.length,
33:       total,
34:       limit,
35:       offset,
36:       runs: paginated,
37:     });
38:   } catch (error) {
39:     return NextResponse.json(
40:       { error: 'Failed to fetch runs', details: String(error) },
41:       { status: 500 }
42:     );
43:   }
44: }

(End of file - total 44 lines)
</content>

1: import { describe, it, expect } from 'vitest';
2: import type { RunDetail } from '../../src/lib/schemas';
3: import { filterRuns, computeStats, exportToCSV, type QueryFilters } from '../../src/lib/export';
4: 
5: function createMockRun(id: string, model: string, runDate: string, totalScore: number, taskId?: string): RunDetail {
6:   return {
7:     id,
8:     model,
9:     harness: 'opencode',
10:     harness_version: '1.0.0',
11:     benchmark_version: '1.0',
12:     run_date: runDate,
13:     wall_time_seconds: 100,
14:     total_score: totalScore,
15:     scores: {
16:       functional_correctness: Math.round(totalScore * 40),
17:       integration_quality: Math.round(totalScore * 25),
18:       regression_safety: Math.round(totalScore * 20),
19:       minimality: Math.round(totalScore * 10),
20:       process_quality: Math.round(totalScore * 5),
21:     },
22:     test_results: [],
23:     artifacts: [],
24:     task_id: taskId,
25:   };
26: }
27: 
28: describe('Query Filtering', () => {
29:   const runs: RunDetail[] = [
30:     createMockRun('run-1', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-01', 0.85, 'task-1'),
31:     createMockRun('run-2', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-05', 0.75, 'task-2'),
32:     createMockRun('run-3', 'claude-3', '2026-04-01', 0.90, 'task-1'),
33:     createMockRun('run-4', 'claude-3', '2026-04-10', 0.88, 'task-3'),
34:     createMockRun('run-5', 'gpt-4o', '2026-03-01', 0.70, 'task-1'),
35:   ];
36: 
37:   it('should filter by model', () => {
38:     const filters: QueryFilters = { model: 'gpt-4o' };
39:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
40:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
41:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.model === 'gpt-4o')).toBe(true);
42:   });
43: 
44:   it('should filter by task', () => {
45:     const filters: QueryFilters = { task: 'task-1' };
46:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
47:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
48:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.task_id === 'task-1')).toBe(true);
49:   });
50: 
51:   it('should filter by date range', () => {
52:     const filters: QueryFilters = {
53:       startDate: '2026-04-01',
54:       endDate: '2026-04-05',
55:     };
56:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
57:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
58:   });
59: 
60:   it('should filter by score range', () => {
61:     const filters: QueryFilters = {
62:       minScore: 0.80,
63:       maxScore: 0.90,
64:     };
65:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
66:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(3);
67:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.total_score >= 0.80 && r.total_score <= 0.90)).toBe(true);
68:   });
69: 
70:   it('should combine multiple filters', () => {
71:     const filters: QueryFilters = {
72:       model: 'gpt-4o',
73:       minScore: 0.75,
74:     };
75:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, filters);
76:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(2);
77:     expect(filtered.every(r => r.model === 'gpt-4o' && r.total_score >= 0.75)).toBe(true);
78:   });
79: 
80:   it('should return all runs when no filters provided', () => {
81:     const filtered = filterRuns(runs, {});
82:     expect(filtered).toHaveLength(runs.length);
83:   });
84: });
85: 
86: describe('Summary Statistics', () => {
87:   it('should compute mean correctly', () => {
88:     const scores = [0.80, 0.85, 0.90, 0.75];
89:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
90:     expect(stats.mean).toBeCloseTo(0.825, 3);
91:   });
92: 
93:   it('should compute median correctly for odd count', () => {
94:     const scores = [0.80, 0.90, 0.70];
95:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
96:     expect(stats.median).toBeCloseTo(0.80, 3);
97:   });
98: 
99:   it('should compute median correctly for even count', () => {
100:     const scores = [0.80, 0.90, 0.70, 0.85];
101:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
102:     expect(stats.median).toBeCloseTo(0.825, 3);
103:   });
104: 
105:   it('should compute p95 correctly', () => {
106:     const scores = Array.from({ length: 100 }, (_, i) => i / 100);
107:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
108:     expect(stats.p95).toBeCloseTo(0.94, 2);
109:   });
110: 
111:   it('should handle single score', () => {
112:     const scores = [0.85];
113:     const stats = computeStats(scores);
114:     expect(stats.mean).toBe(stats.median);
115:     expect(stats.p95).toBe(0.85);
116:   });
117: });
118: 
119: describe('CSV Export', () => {
120:   const runs: RunDetail[] = [
121:     createMockRun('run-1', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-01', 0.85, 'task-1'),
122:     createMockRun('run-2', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-05', 0.75, 'task-2'),
123:   ];
124: 
125:   it('should generate valid CSV with headers', () => {
126:     const csv = exportToCSV(runs);
127:     const lines = csv.split('\n');
128:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('id');
129:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('model');
130:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('total_score');
131:   });
132: 
133:   it('should include all run data rows', () => {
134:     const csv = exportToCSV(runs);
135:     const lines = csv.split('\n').filter(l => l.length > 0);
136:     expect(lines.length).toBe(runs.length + 1);
137:   });
138: 
139:   it('should escape CSV special characters', () => {
140:     const runsWithSpecial: RunDetail[] = [
141:       { ...createMockRun('run-1', 'gpt-4o', '2026-04-01', 0.85), model: 'gpt-4o, mini' },
142:     ];
143:     const csv = exportToCSV(runsWithSpecial);
144:     expect(csv).toContain('"gpt-4o, mini"');
145:   });
146: 
147:   it('should handle empty array', () => {
148:     const csv = exportToCSV([]);
149:     const lines = csv.split('\n').filter(l => l.length > 0);
150:     expect(lines.length).toBe(1);
151:     expect(lines[0]).toContain('id');
152:   });
153: });

(End of file - total 153 lines)
</content>

70:   },
71:   {
72:     id: 'run-3',
73:     model: 'claude-3',
74:     provider: 'anthropic',
75:     harness: 'opencode',
76:     harness_version: '1.0.0',
77:     benchmark_version: '1.0',
78:     run_date: '2026-04-03',
79:     wall_time_seconds: 90,
80:     total_score: 0.90,
81:     scores: {
82:       functional_correctness: 36,
83:       integration_quality: 22,
84:       regression_safety: 18,

92: 
93: describe('API Routes', () => {
94:   beforeEach(() => {
95:     vi.clearAllMocks();
96:   });
97: 
98:   describe('GET /api/runs', () => {
99:     it('should return all runs when no filters provided', async () => {
100:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
101:       vi.mocked(getAllRuns).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns as any);
102: 
103:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs');
104:       const response = await runsHandler(request);
105:       const data = await response.json();
106: 
107:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
108:       expect(data.count).toBe(3);
109:       expect(data.runs).toHaveLength(3);
110:     });
111: 
112:     it('should filter runs by model', async () => {
113:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
114:       vi.mocked(getAllRuns).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns as any);
115: 
116:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs?model=gpt-4o');
117:       const response = await runsHandler(request);
118:       const data = await response.json();
119: 
120:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
121:       expect(data.count).toBe(2);
122:       expect(data.runs.every((r: { model: string }) => r.model === 'gpt-4o')).toBe(true);
123:     });
124: 
125:     it('should filter runs by score range', async () => {
126:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
127:       vi.mocked(getAllRuns).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns as any);
128: 
129:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs?minScore=0.80&maxScore=0.95');
130:       const response = await runsHandler(request);
131:       const data = await response.json();
132: 
133:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
134:       expect(data.count).toBe(2);
135:     });
136:   });
137: 
138:   describe('GET /api/leaderboard', () => {
139:     it('should return leaderboard with stats sorted by mean score', async () => {
140:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
141:       vi.mocked(getAllRuns).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns as any);
142: 
143:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/leaderboard');
144:       const response = await leaderboardHandler(request);
145:       const data = await response.json();
146: 
147:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
148:       expect(data.count).toBe(2);
149:       expect(data.leaderboard[0].model).toBe('claude-3');
150:       expect(data.leaderboard[0].meanScore).toBeCloseTo(0.90, 2);
151:     });
152: 
153:     it('should filter leaderboard by provider', async () => {
154:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
155:       vi.mocked(getAllRuns).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns as any);
156: 
157:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/leaderboard?provider=openai');
158:       const response = await leaderboardHandler(request);
159:       const data = await response.json();
160: 
161:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
162:       expect(data.count).toBe(1);
163:       expect(data.leaderboard[0].model).toBe('gpt-4o');
164:     });
165:   });
166: 
167:   describe('GET /api/runs/[id]', () => {
168:     it('should return a single run by id', async () => {
169:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
170:       vi.mocked(getRunById).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns[0] as any);
171: 
172:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs/run-1');
173:       const response = await runsIdHandler(request, { params: Promise.resolve({ id: 'run-1' }) });
174:       const data = await response.json();
175: 
176:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
177:       expect(data.id).toBe('run-1');
178:       expect(data.model).toBe('gpt-4o');
179:     });
180: 
181:     it('should return 404 for non-existent run', async () => {
182:       vi.mocked(getRunById).mockResolvedValue(null);
183: 
184:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs/non-existent');
185:       const response = await runsIdHandler(request, { params: Promise.resolve({ id: 'non-existent' }) });
186: 
187:       expect(response.status).toBe(404);
188:     });
189:   });
190: 
191:   describe('GET /api/runs pagination', () => {
192:     it('should paginate runs with limit and offset', async () => {
193:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
194:       vi.mocked(getAllRuns).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns as any);
195: 
196:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs?limit=2&offset=0');
197:       const response = await runsHandler(request);
198:       const data = await response.json();
199: 
200:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
201:       expect(data.count).toBe(2);
202:       expect(data.runs).toHaveLength(2);
203:       expect(data.total).toBe(3);
204:       expect(data.limit).toBe(2);
205:       expect(data.offset).toBe(0);
206:     });
207: 
208:     it('should return empty array when offset exceeds data', async () => {
209:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
210:       vi.mocked(getAllRuns).mockResolvedValue(mockRuns as any);
211: 
212:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs?limit=10&offset=100');
213:       const response = await runsHandler(request);
214:       const data = await response.json();
215: 
216:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
217:       expect(data.runs).toHaveLength(0);
218:     });
219:   });
220: 
221:   describe('GET /api/tasks', () => {
222:     const mockTasks = [
223:       { id: 'task-1', title: 'Task 1', difficulty: 'easy', description: 'Desc 1', version: '1.0', status: 'published' as const },
224:       { id: 'task-2', title: 'Task 2', difficulty: 'medium', description: 'Desc 2', version: '1.0', status: 'published' as const },
225:       { id: 'task-3', title: 'Task 3', difficulty: 'hard', description: 'Desc 3', version: '1.0', status: 'published' as const },
226:     ];
227: 
228:     it('should return all tasks when no filters provided', async () => {
229:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
230:       vi.mocked(getTasks).mockResolvedValue(mockTasks as any);
231: 
232:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/tasks');
233:       const response = await tasksHandler(request);
234:       const data = await response.json();
235: 
236:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
237:       expect(data.count).toBe(3);
238:       expect(data.tasks).toHaveLength(3);
239:     });
240: 
241:     it('should filter tasks by difficulty', async () => {
242:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
243:       vi.mocked(getTasks).mockResolvedValue(mockTasks as any);
244: 
245:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/tasks?difficulty=medium');
246:       const response = await tasksHandler(request);
247:       const data = await response.json();
248: 
249:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
250:       expect(data.count).toBe(1);
251:       expect(data.tasks[0].id).toBe('task-2');
252:     });
253:   });
254: 
255:   describe('GET /api/tasks/[id]', () => {
256:     const mockTasks = [
257:       { id: 'task-1', title: 'Task 1', difficulty: 'easy', description: 'Desc 1', version: '1.0', status: 'published' as const },
258:     ];
259: 
260:     it('should return a single task by id', async () => {
261:       // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
262:       vi.mocked(getTaskById).mockResolvedValue(mockTasks[0] as any);
263: 
264:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/tasks/task-1');
265:       const response = await tasksIdHandler(request, { params: Promise.resolve({ id: 'task-1' }) });
266:       const data = await response.json();
267: 
268:       expect(response.status).toBe(200);
269:       expect(data.id).toBe('task-1');
270:       expect(data.title).toBe('Task 1');
271:     });
272: 
273:     it('should return 404 for non-existent task', async () => {
274:       vi.mocked(getTaskById).mockResolvedValue(undefined);
275: 
276:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/tasks/non-existent');
277:       const response = await tasksIdHandler(request, { params: Promise.resolve({ id: 'non-existent' }) });
278: 
279:       expect(response.status).toBe(404);
280:     });
281:   });
282: 
283:   describe('Auth Requirements', () => {
284:     it('should reject requests without valid API key', async () => {
285:       vi.mocked(validateAuth).mockResolvedValueOnce({ allowed: false, error: 'Invalid API key', status: 401 });
286: 
287:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs');
288:       const response = await runsHandler(request);
289: 
290:       expect(response.status).toBe(401);
291:     });
292: 
293:     it('should reject requests that exceed rate limit', async () => {
294:       vi.mocked(validateAuth).mockResolvedValueOnce({ allowed: false, error: 'Rate limit exceeded', status: 429 });
295: 
296:       const request = new NextRequest('http://localhost/api/runs');
297:       const response = await runsHandler(request);
298: 
299:       expect(response.status).toBe(429);
300:     });
301:   });
302: });

part.text:
All 34 tests pass. Here's the summary of changes:

**`src/lib/export.ts`** — Added `datasetVersion?: string` to `QueryFilters` and a filter clause in `filterRuns` that matches against `run.dataset_version`.

**`app/api/runs/route.ts`** — Added parsing of the `datasetVersion` query param into the filters object.

**`tests/lib/export.test.ts`** — Updated `createMockRun` to accept `datasetVersion`, added it to test fixtures, and added two tests: datasetVersion-only filtering and datasetVersion + model combined.

**`tests/app/api/routes.test.ts`** — Added `dataset_version` to mock runs and added two route-level tests: datasetVersion filtering and datasetVersion + model combined.
